standards_passed: no-inline-code (both files are pure auto-generated index/reference documentation; the code block in INDEX.md is a directory tree display using box-drawing characters, not executable logic)
no-hardcoded-values (PASS — markdown metadata contains generated literals, but these files are not scripts and contain no runtime values requiring parameterization)
use-source-venv (no python3 calls anywhere in either file)
integer-step-numbering (no step numbering present in either file)
meaningful-naming (INDEX.md and README.md are standard convention names, not generic enhancement words)
english-only (all content is English; non-ASCII characters are em-dashes U+2014, valid English punctuation; no Chinese or non-English language text found)
merge-similar-scripts (both files are .md documentation, not scripts; not applicable)

concise-documentation (both files are terse, auto-generated index/reference files; no verbose prose, marketing language, or repetitive examples; duplicate auto-generated footer in README.md is structural boilerplate not subject to Standard 8 which targets human-authored verbose documentation)
parameterized-scripts (both files are .md documentation, not scripts; not applicable)
dangling-references (both files list command filenames in human-readable descriptions, not as script invocations; no script path references requiring resolution)
merge-conflicts (no conflict markers found in either file)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 2 issues with proposed fixes and 1 observation. (1) Alleged concise-documentation violation for duplicate footer in README.md lines 50/55. (2) Wording clarification on no-hardcoded-values PASS rationale. (3) OBSERVATION_ONLY: convention field inconsistency between INDEX.md ('kebab') and README.md ('lower').

findings.issue: Missed concise-documentation violation in commands/README.md:50 and commands/README.md:55 — auto-generated footer appears twice, alleged redundant documentation bloat.
findings.proposed_fix: Remove the duplicate footer inside the AUTO block at commands/README.md:50, leaving only the final footer at commands/README.md:55.
findings.rationale: Standard 8 targets human-authored verbose documentation: non-functional descriptive text, marketing language, repetitive examples, verbose multi-paragraph explanations, pseudocode. Both footer instances are single-line terse auto-generated notes from the doc-sync hook. Structural auto-generated boilerplate duplication is not a Standard 8 trigger. Rejected as out_of_scope for this audit.

findings.issue: Draft no-hardcoded-values wording is slightly inaccurate — files do contain generated literals but this is still PASS because the standard applies to scripts/runtime values.
findings.proposed_fix: Change wording to: 'PASS — markdown metadata contains generated literals, but these files are not scripts and contain no runtime values requiring parameterization.'
findings.rationale: Wording improvement accepted and reflected in standards_passed text. No change to violation count.

findings.issue: commands/INDEX.md:5 says convention is 'kebab' while commands/README.md:10 says 'lower'. Inconsistency between the two auto-generated files.
findings.rationale: Codex explicitly marked this OBSERVATION_ONLY with no proposed fix. The 11 development standards do not include a standard for naming-convention metadata consistency in auto-generated index files. Logged but not actioned.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied wording clarification to no-hardcoded-values PASS rationale. Rejected duplicate-footer finding as out_of_scope for Standard 8. Logged convention inconsistency observation.
